title: Automate Expiration of Blood Units Older than 42 Days
description: The system shall automatically flag or deactivate blood units that have a collection date more than 42 days ago.
rationale: To ensure the safety and efficacy of blood transfusions by preventing the use of outdated blood.
business_value: Reduces manual checking and human error, enhances patient safety, and ensures compliance with regulatory standards.

source.description: As a blood bank administrator I need blood units older than 42 days to be automatically expired So that outdated blood is not mistakenly used for transfusions

acceptance_criteria: Given a blood unit has a collection date
When the current date is more than 42 days after the collection date
Then the blood unit is automatically marked as expired
preconditions: The system must have access to the current date and time.
The system must store the collection date of each blood unit.
postconditions: The status of the blood unit is updated to 'expired'.
The unit should no longer be available for transfusion or use.
functional_constraints: The system must be able to calculate the difference between the current date and the collection date.
The system must update the status of the blood unit within a specified time frame (e.g., 10 minutes).

performance.throughput: At least 10,000 units processed per hour during peak hours.

scalability.concurrency: Supports at least 50 concurrent users during peak usage times.
scalability.growth: Can scale to handle an increase of 50% in the number of blood units within a year.

usability.error_handling: Should provide clear and concise error messages.
usability.interface_behavior: The system should automatically refresh the status of units daily at midnight.

edge_cases: What happens if there is a system clock failure?
How does the system handle blood units that were collected on exactly 42 days ago?
What if the collection date is not recorded for a blood unit?
dependencies: A reliable method to obtain the current date and time.
Storage of all blood units with their respective collection dates.

interfaces.name: Blood Units Database
interfaces.description: The system will interact with this database to retrieve and update the collection dates and statuses of blood units.

interfaces.name: System Clock API
interfaces.description: This API provides real-time date and time, which is used by the system to calculate the age of blood units.

test_cases.description: Verify that a blood unit with a collection date exactly 42 days ago is automatically marked as expired.
test_cases.expected_result: The system marks the blood unit as expired and updates its status accordingly.

test_cases.description: Simulate a system clock failure to ensure the system can handle such an edge case without crashing.
test_cases.expected_result: The system continues to operate normally and logs an error message for further investigation.
